name: Virginia
tooltip: Virginia — Cat 3: Va. Code § 8.01-40. 20-year post-mortem. No AI-specific overlay yet.
rightOfPublicity: Yes — Va. Code § 8.01-40. Covers unauthorized commercial use of name, portrait, or picture. Functions as a right of publicity per *Town & Country Properties, Inc. v. Riggins*, 457 S.E.2d 356 (Va. 1995).
postMortem: Yes — 20 years after death. Va. Code § 8.01-40(B).
remedy: Civil suit under § 8.01-40 for actual damages plus injunctive relief and exemplary damages where the use was knowing.
whatsInteresting: Virginia's 20-year post-mortem term is the shortest among states with codified post-mortem rights. The statute is also narrower than most — name, portrait, picture only, no voice, no signature. *Riggins* (the Washington football player case) is the case that established Virginia's statute reaches commercial-value claims, not just dignity-based privacy. For most working entertainers, Virginia is a state where you'd want to forum-shop into a stronger jurisdiction if you have the choice.
primarySources: *Town & Country Properties, Inc. v. Riggins*, 457 S.E.2d 356 (Va. 1995)
